name: gmail-blade-mcp
title: Google Gmail
description: Gmail email operations via Google API — search, read, send, threads, labels, drafts, filters. Gemini-powered classification and summarisation.
author: groupthink-dev

highlights.text: Search & threads — Gmail query syntax, thread grouping, label filtering
Send & draft — compose, reply, forward, save drafts for review
Labels & filters — organise mail with labels and automated filter rules
Gemini AI — on-device classification and summarisation via Gemini Flash
OAuth 2.0 — standard Google consent flow, no stored passwords
Write-gated — reads always work, sends require explicit opt-in

readme:
### What it does

Gmail Blade gives your AI agent full access to a Gmail account through the official Google API. Tools cover search, reading, sending, thread management, labels, drafts, and filters. An optional Gemini integration adds on-device email classification and summarisation using Google's lightweight Gemini 2.0 Flash Lite model.

Your agent can search by Gmail query syntax, read full message content, manage labels, create draft replies for human review, and set up filter rules — all through a clean tool interface.

### Gemini integration

When a `GOOGLE_API_KEY` is provided, Gmail Blade can classify incoming messages by priority and category, and generate concise summaries of long threads. This runs via the Gemini API — not the host LLM — so it works regardless of which model is driving the agent. The Gemini features are optional; all core email operations work without an API key.

### Safety model

All tools follow a read-free, write-gated pattern:

- **Reads** work immediately — search, read messages, list labels, view threads
- **Writes** require explicit opt-in — send, reply, create drafts, modify labels, create filters

### Setup

Gmail Blade uses OAuth 2.0 with the standard Google consent flow. You create a Cloud project, enable the Gmail API, download OAuth credentials, and complete a one-time browser-based authorisation. No passwords are stored — the refresh token handles subsequent sessions.

env.name: GOOGLE_API_KEY
env.description: Google API key for Gemini AI features (optional — classification and summarisation)

setup.help_text: 1. Create a project at Google Cloud Console. 2. Enable the Gmail API. 3. Create OAuth 2.0 credentials (Desktop app type). 4. Download the JSON and save as ~/.gmail-blade/credentials.json. 5. On first run, complete the OAuth consent flow in your browser.
prerequisites: Google Cloud project with Gmail API enabled

scenarios.label: Classify and triage unread mail
scenarios.body: Search for unread messages, use Gemini classification to sort by priority, label high-priority items for immediate attention, and archive low-priority newsletters.

scenarios.label: Draft replies for a thread
scenarios.body: Read a multi-message thread, generate a context-aware draft reply, and save it for human review before sending.

scenarios.label: Set up inbox automation with filters
scenarios.body: Analyse recent mail patterns, create filter rules to auto-label recurring senders, and verify the filters are working on new incoming messages.
